premise: My mother’s $310,000 rare‑stamp collection was held in trust by my uncle and aunt until I turned 50, but they secretly sold the entire collection to a dealer and spent the cash on a luxury RV, telling me it had been ‘lost in a flood.’ At 54, I found a catalog notation from the auction, traced the stamps to a collector in Zurich, successfully sued for conversion, and repossessed the collection; I then sold individual stamps at a 40% premium to fund a women’s entrepreneurship grant, with each award certificate permanently embossed with my uncle’s name as the unwilling donor.
